audit_items.proof_trace: The only heap allocation in ProgramMain() is `char* data` returned by read_file(); it is freed exactly once at line 134 (delete[] data) after all uses complete. All BinaryReaderIR callbacks convert string_view pointers (which point into the data buffer) to owned std::string copies via .to_string(), and OnDataSegmentData uses memcpy into a std::vector — so Module holds no raw pointers into data and its destructor cannot access the freed buffer.
